payload.title: MTA Board Action Items
payload.description: MTA Board Meeting packet for March 27, 2024, including Joint Board Meeting Minutes from February 28, 2024, CBDTP Toll Rate Schedule, Procurement Actions, Real Estate Items, and Corporate Governance items.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: The Board of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority met as the Boards of its constituent agencies on March 27, 2024 at 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM ET at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY 10004

tool_args.label: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (TBTA / MTA Bridges and Tunnels)

payload.description: The legal name of MTA Bridges and Tunnels is Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority. Operates the Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP).

MTA Board Meeting at 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room New York, NY 10004. Agenda included: Public Comment Period, Executive Session, Presentations, Approval of Minutes, CBDTP Toll Rate Schedule, Committee on Capital Program, Committee on Metro-North Railroad & LIRR, Committee on NYCT & Bus, Committee on MTA Bridges & Tunnels, Committee on Finance, Committee on Corporate Governance.

Board adopted the CBD Charge Schedule (Part 1021.4 of Title 21 NYCRR) for the Central Business District Tolling Program operated by TBTA/MTA Bridges and Tunnels. Scheduled for implementation in or about June 2024.

payload.event_name: MTA and MTA Agencies Regular Joint Committee and Board Meeting
payload.description: Joint Board Meeting. Minutes from February 28, 2024 were presented for approval at the March 27, 2024 meeting.

payload.raw_value_text: CBD toll rates for vehicles using fare media other than E-ZPass, commonly referred to as Tolls by Mail, would be 50% more than their respective E-ZPass rates
payload.description: Tolls by Mail rates are 50% more than the E-ZPass rate

tool_args.label: CBDTP Net Annual Revenue Target for Capital

payload.raw_value_text: generate enough net annual revenue to fund $15 billion for MTA's transit and commuter rail system capital projects
payload.description: CBDTP is required to generate enough net annual revenue to fund $15 billion for MTA's transit and commuter rail system capital projects as required by law

tool_args.label: CBDTP Passenger Vehicle Peak E-ZPass Toll

payload.raw_value_text: $15 peak period ($3.75 overnight period) E-ZPass toll for entering the CBD, no more than once per day
payload.description: CBDTP E-ZPass toll rate for passenger vehicles entering the CBD

extra_fields.vehicle_class: Passenger vehicles and passenger-type vehicles with commercial license plates

tool_args.label: CBDTP Passenger Vehicle Overnight E-ZPass Toll

payload.raw_value_text: $3.75 overnight period E-ZPass toll
payload.description: CBDTP E-ZPass toll rate for passenger vehicles in overnight period

extra_fields.vehicle_class: Passenger vehicles and passenger-type vehicles with commercial license plates

payload.raw_value_text: $7.50 peak period E-ZPass toll for entering the CBD, no more than once per day
payload.description: CBDTP E-ZPass toll for motorcycles peak period

tool_args.label: CBDTP Single-Unit Truck Peak E-ZPass Toll

payload.raw_value_text: $24 peak period ($6 overnight) E-ZPass toll for entering the CBD
payload.description: CBDTP E-ZPass toll for single-unit trucks peak period

tool_args.label: CBDTP Multi-Unit Truck Peak E-ZPass Toll

payload.raw_value_text: $36 peak period ($9 overnight) E-ZPass toll for entering the CBD
payload.description: CBDTP E-ZPass toll for multi-unit trucks peak period

payload.raw_value_text: $1.25 per-trip for taxis, green cabs and FHVs
payload.description: Per-trip CBD toll for taxi, green cab, and FHV passenger trips to/from/within/through the CBD

extra_fields.vehicle_class: NYC TLC taxis, green cabs, for-hire vehicles

payload.raw_value_text: $2.50 per-trip for FHVs on trips dispatched by a high-volume for-hire service (HVHFS)
payload.description: Per-trip CBD toll for high-volume for-hire service dispatched trips

extra_fields.vehicle_class: FHVs dispatched by high-volume for-hire services

tool_args.label: CBDTP Passenger Vehicle Peak Tolls by Mail Rate

payload.raw_value_text: $22.50 peak period for passenger vehicles using non-E-ZPass media
payload.description: CBDTP Tolls by Mail peak rate for passenger vehicles

payload.raw_value_text: $5 tunnel crossing credit for passenger vehicles entering the CBD via the Lincoln or Holland Tunnel
payload.description: E-ZPass tunnel crossing credit for passenger vehicles entering CBD via Lincoln or Holland Tunnel

tool_args.label: Passenger Vehicle Tunnel Credit QMT/Hugh Carey

payload.raw_value_text: $2.50 tunnel crossing credit for passenger vehicles entering or exiting the CBD via the Queens-Midtown or Hugh L. Carey Tunnel
payload.description: E-ZPass tunnel crossing credit for passenger vehicles via Queens-Midtown or Hugh L. Carey Tunnel

extra_fields.tunnel: Queens-Midtown Tunnel, Hugh L. Carey Tunnel

payload.raw_value_text: Toll rates would be 75% lower in the overnight period
payload.description: Overnight period toll rates are 75% lower than peak period

payload.raw_value_text: $24.00 peak period for buses ($6 overnight)
payload.description: CBDTP E-ZPass toll for buses peak period

payload.raw_value_text: $36.00 peak period for licensed sightseeing buses ($9 overnight)
payload.description: CBDTP E-ZPass toll for licensed sightseeing buses peak period

tool_args.label: Governor Hochul Budget Avoids Fiscal Cliff

payload.claim_text: Chair Lieber applauded Governor Hochul and the State Legislature for passing a historic budget that enabled the MTA to avoid the fiscal cliff and allowed the agency to maintain and increase service without service reductions or layoffs.
payload.subject: MTA fiscal cliff
payload.description: Governor Hochul and State Legislature budget allowed MTA to avoid fiscal cliff and maintain/increase service without reductions or layoffs.

payload.claim_text: Chair Lieber stated that once congestion pricing is enacted and implemented, the Board will be asked to approve the next Capital Program by the end of the year. Chair Lieber highlighted the need to prioritize mass transit commuters and promote cleaner air and safer streets through congestion pricing.
payload.subject: congestion pricing implementation
payload.description: Once congestion pricing is enacted, Board will be asked to approve next Capital Program by end of year.

payload.claim_text: Chair Lieber acknowledged MTA's efforts to improve and expand services, including reductions in wait times and increases in train frequency in anticipation of congestion pricing going into effect. The Chair highlighted the investments made in the bus service redesign and expansion, particularly in areas like Queens.
payload.subject: bus service redesign
payload.description: MTA investments in bus service redesign and expansion in Queens

tool_args.label: Future Projects Penn Station Access and Second Avenue Subway

payload.claim_text: Chair Lieber discussed future projects like the Metro-North Penn Station Access project and the Second Avenue Subway Phase Two project, aimed at enhancing accessibility and serving transit-dependent communities.
payload.subject: future capital projects
payload.description: Metro-North Penn Station Access and Second Avenue Subway Phase Two projects mentioned as future projects for enhancing accessibility

payload.claim_text: The Chair described safety in the subway system as a crucial equity issue, and he emphasized the need for a safe environment for riders and MTA employees.
payload.subject: subway safety
payload.description: Safety in subway system described as a crucial equity issue

payload.claim_text: School buses contracted with the NYC Department of Education, commuter vans licensed with the NYC Taxi and Limousine Commission, and buses providing scheduled commuter services open to the public would be exempted from the CBD toll. Specialized government vehicles, qualifying authorized emergency vehicles and qualifying vehicles transporting persons with disabilities would be exempt from the CBD toll, as required by law.
payload.subject: CBDTP exemptions
payload.description: Categories of vehicles exempt from CBD tolls

tool_args.label: CBDTP Adaptive Management and Variable Rate

payload.claim_text: CBD entry charges and tunnel credits are subject to a variable percentage increase/decrease of up to 10% for up to one year after implementation pursuant to the adaptive management approach. The Authority reserves the right to charge a 25% higher CBD charge during Gridlock Alert Days.
payload.subject: CBDTP adaptive management
payload.description: CBDTP rates subject to adaptive management (up to 10% variable adjustment) and 25% increase on Gridlock Alert Days
CBDTP is operated by TBTA (MTA Bridges and Tunnels)
MTA Board Meeting on March 27, 2024 included adoption of CBDTP Toll Rate Schedule
CBDTP to generate $15 billion for MTA capital projects
Minutes from February 28, 2024 MTA Board meeting were presented for approval at March 27 meeting
